codeReview.summary: Reviewed the feature handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit `10a0253` for the special-moves/legality implementation. The diff adds square-attack and check detection, absolute pin detection, pseudo-legal castling/en-passant/promotion generation, legal-move filtering via make/unmake, mate/stalemate helpers, a reversible state stack on `Position`, and a focused legality test suite covering the requested scenarios. I did not find any blocking or non-blocking correctness defects in the reviewed feature scope.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `engine-worker` skill is stale relative to this repository layout. It still tells workers to read/write tests under `tests/`, edit sources under `engine/`, and compile with `engine/*.c tests/*.c`, while the actual Claudefish engine layout and commands use `engine/src/*.c` and `engine/tests/*.c`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/engine-worker/SKILL.md:36,42,51,63` vs mission `AGENTS.md:39,45-46,58`. The reviewed worker's transcript skeleton/handoff also show the real paths: reads from `engine/src/position.h` and `engine/tests/test_main.c`, plus added coverage in `engine/tests/test_legality.c` (`handoffs/...8b2b6da0-f5d0-46f5-bd11-0e37f52e15da.json:11,45`).

summary: Pass. The reviewed commit fully implements the scoped special-move legality work and backs it with targeted legality/integrity tests. I did not find feature-scoped code defects, but I noted a stale engine-worker skill that still points future workers at the wrong engine source/test paths.
